title: Why Particle Physics Became a Victim of Its Own Success - Adam Brown
summary: Adam Brown discusses how particle physics has faced challenges despite historical success, suggesting the field has encountered structural or strategic difficulties as a consequence of its own achievements. The conversation explores why progress in the field has become harder or less productive despite its foundational importance.
writeup: Adam Brown argues that particle physics has run into difficulty as a direct consequence of its past success. The conversation examines why progress in the field has grown harder and less productive even as the field remains foundationally important, framing the slowdown as a structural or strategic problem rather than a failure of ambition. The core takeaway: the achievements that established particle physics also created the conditions that now constrain it.
